This is going to be a quick video on CBOs or Campaign Budget Optimization for Facebook that Facebook will be making most of a switch to in the near future. So just a quick understanding of what it is. Campaign Budget Optimization is a way of optimizing the distribution of campaign budget across your campaigns ad sets. This means Facebook is going to automatically and continuously find the best available opportunities for your results across your ad sets and distributes your campaign budget in real time to get those results. So kind of similar to how you have, if you have multiple ads underneath an ad set and how Facebook determines which ad is the right ad to show to the right person, you can do this at the higher level with Campaign Budget Optimization. Facebook determines which ad sets even allowed to spend money based off of things like CPMs and cost per click and all of those things and optimizing more towards your actual goal here. So it's actually been pretty successful for me, so let's just jump into how you build out a CBO campaign. Let me start this over here. So here I am in my ad account. You're just going to click Create and we'll press Start over here. I don't use this guided creation very often, so I'm going to hop to Quick Creation and I'm just going to give myself a campaign name of TestCBO. And so by default nowadays, this is already, the CBO option is already turned on. Oops, I need to change my campaign objective. It is going to be conversions because I'm going to run this to a funnel. So back to what I was saying, by default nowadays Facebook just automatically turns this on. And what's interesting is we've all been always taught to turn this off, right? And so then we can set budgets at the ad set level. Now we're going to set budgets at the campaign level. So this is where you have to know how many ads you're going to be placing inside of your campaign or how many ad sets you're going to be placing inside of your campaign. And then of course what your typical daily spending on an ad is. What do you allow? Do you allow $10? Do you allow $20? In my case, I start all of my ads out at $50 and I used to let them scale right from $50 up and higher. So at the very least I would want to start my budget somewhere in that $50 per ad. So let's say I'm going to put 20 ads in here. If I'm going to put 20 ads in this campaign, well then at 20 ads times 50, I'm going to need $1,000 for daily budget. And so that's what I'm essentially saying I'm allowed to spend. And so this is an interesting thing to note because you can no longer change your budget on the ad set level when you're using the CBO. And so it comes about scaling your actual campaign budget, not scaling an ad set budget. We're going to hop in here and we're just going to name a, we're going to create a new ad set really quickly. Test CBO. Let's call it something for real top. Let's do all of this for top of funnel. Test CBO. GL. Oh, no, this is going to be, this is CBO. So test, test video, CBO. And so I just referenced that this is top of funnel. This is what the campaign is. And then this is the type of campaign it is. It's just a CBO campaign. Then my ad set name is going to be top of funnel one year later. And then I'm going to choose an audience or an interest group that I have saved. So if you're not doing interest group targeting or audience targeting, you don't need to worry about this part. But this part is super awesome that Facebook has just gotten way more dynamic with us. So I'm just going to say this will be MMA, AUD, because I have an audience called MMA, AUD. And I'm going to literally create my ad ad ad name to be the same. So top of funnel one year later. Oops, let's make them the same. And then we're going to go ahead and save the draft. It's going to pop us open our test CBO campaign so we can again see what we're looking to do is we're going to do conversions. We're buying type of auction. We've set our spending limit of $1,000. And then we have campaign budget optimization on. Show advanced options. You can schedule these things, but you can set that within each ad set. So we're going to go ahead and hop into the ad set. And we're going to choose our conversion event. While we're not looking for ad to cart, we are looking for, let's just say GL or not. Let's do our new funnel. GL New Case Study 2 apps. So this is just a custom conversion that we've set up for in one of our funnels. And scroll down. We can again choose the time. I'm just going to choose something ridiculous. So that way I don't accidentally turn anything on. So when we come into our audience selection, so if you're not doing detailed demographic targeting, so you've not come down and you've built out lists like this, right, of interest groups, which you can, right, fitness. And then you can select, you know, at least 50 of your interest groups if you're not doing that. And saving that as a custom audience, or you don't have customer lists uploaded and look like audience is created, then you can kind of skip this part and really the rest of the video. But in my case, I'm going to use a saved audience. And I'm going to use a look-alike audience. I'm going to use a look-alike audience. And I'm going to select my, I said it was going to be the MMA audience. So I should have a look-alike for MMA. And there it is. And so now I have my audience selected. I'm just going to go ahead and build out my demographic, right, whatever I'm looking for. I'm not going to do any more detailed targeting because my audience has all of that built into it, right. I'm going to go ahead and select edit my placements. And then again, choose your normal placements that you use that have been effective for you. For me, it's Facebook feed, stories, Instagram feed and stories. I always turn off audience network. I've not had good results there. And I tend to turn off messenger stories and just run inbox message. So that is my stuff. My conversion is seven days to click. Of course, you can't change any of the rest of the stuff. We're going to go ahead and hop into the ad. You would go ahead and build out your ad. I use an existing post, right. So I know almost all of my ads. We have our Facebook posts uploaded to the web. And I select my content, right. This might not be the content that's relevant to this ad. I'm not actually going to publish this. That said, you can see, so now I've built out an ad and I'm telling it to track. And I've got my pixel installed and everything like this, right. So we have the CBO. We have the first ad set up, right. We're going to build out, what did we say, 20 ads. So we can literally, once we have all of the stuff set up, we can duplicate this 20 times, right. And we're going to rename it. We would change it to one year later, Jason or whatever it is, right. So we have each ad set name is labeled uniquely and each ad, of course, is named uniquely. Once you have all 20 of these built out, you're going to change this one. And I know this isn't Jason, but we're just, these aren't even the right ads. I'm just quickly clicking through. It is, it's going to be Jason. Here we go. So I got Jason's one year later here and it is the, even the new model funnel. So awesome. So you can kind of see what it would look like, right. Now once we have all 20 of our ads here, it's important that we, that we actually labeled these ad sets, MMA, AUD, right. We labeled them our audience. And the reason why that's important is because we're not going to publish this in order to get the best out of a CBO. We're going to actually close this, we're going to actually close our, our options here. Actually, before we close our window, we're going to copy our campaign name. And just so we have it, and we're going to close this here, we're going to press close. We don't want to publish anything yet. We're going to actually go ahead and do a campaign name filter inside of Facebook. So we're just seeing the one campaign and then we're going to come into our ad sets, right. And so you would have your 20 ads or however many ads you, you determined that we're going to go into this audience built or into this campaign built out, right. What we're going to then do is we're going to click all of our ads and we're going to press control D on our keyboard. And that's going to duplicate all of, all of our ad sets. And then it's going to pop us back in here. And so we can see all of our, all of our new ones named MMA, UD slash copy, right. So the new thing that was added to it was copy in the ad set name. So what we're going to do again is close the window. We're not going to publish. We're going to hop into ad set name and we're going to just add the word copy. It contains the word copy. So now we just see all of the copy, right. And what we're going to do is we're going to have all of these ad sets selected. We're going to press the edit button up here. And the reason why we're going to select the edit button up here is because that's going to allow us to just edit these two at the same time. And we're going to scroll down to our audience and we're going to remove the MMA and we're going to say, we're going to choose our other audience. And so this one is going to be normal gems, right. That's just another audience I have. So Alex email normal gems. We have that lookalike audience set. We're not done changing it yet. We're going to sort of close this out again, not publishing. And we're going to go ahead and edit all of these ad sets, right. And we're going to say normal, normal gem audience, NGAUD, right. And I like to just copy that onto my clipboard so then I can quickly click into all of these things, press save to draft, click into all of these things, press save to draft. So what this is now doing, I'm going to go ahead and uncheck the copy because the copy doesn't exist anymore. But what this now is doing is it would essentially have given us 40 ads, right. If we had built out the first 20 initially and then duplicated, it would give us 40 ads. We would adjust the first 20 to go to the new gem owner audience and the other, excuse me, the first 20 went to the MMA audience and now the other 20 would be going to the new gem owner audience. So what Facebook is going to do is dynamically choose how to spend money, not only based off of the creative and the copy and the ad set, it's also going to choose how to spend money based off of which audience is performing better, right. So this will make it start spending less money on the MMA audience if you're not getting conversions there and start spending more money on the new gem owner audience if it's getting conversions there. And so this is a really outstanding way for Facebook to spend our money. That said, there can be a lot of panic in doing these and running these campaigns. The first 24 to 48 hours can look really crazy. And that is because Facebook is having to learn just like in a traditional ad set. It's just having to learn at a higher level. So the big thing here to note is once you publish this, you're not going to want to touch this campaign for 24 to 48 hours. And that is because until it's reached really 48 hours, it's not going to actually be doing its best job to spend your money. So don't panic. Just make certain you're keeping an eye on all of your things. And then of course, as Facebook does start to spend money, I'm just going to discard these drafts so I don't have them. As Facebook does start to spend money on a CBO campaign and I'm just going to hop into the MMA and normal gem CBO, I have running right here. You can see inside of the actual ad sets and how Facebook is choosing to spend money. So if we change it to today and we scroll over and we look at our... Let's do this one because I have with my amount spent over here so we can see it. So you can see if I sort by amount spent, the most typical you're going to see is the amount spent is going to be something that has the lowest cost per click, the better CPMs. You can see this ad was getting stuff spent on it. It didn't get results. I don't think we have to scroll back over. It didn't get results. And so Facebook stopped spending money on this ad because the CPMs were higher and the cost per click was higher and then Facebook started diverting and spending money in other places. And so if you literally if you sit in this tab and where it's just continuously spam refresh, you would watch your amount spent start increasing on things that have little to no spend. And then you would also start seeing it, the amount spent decreasing on the things that it's just getting too high of a cost per result or it's not getting any results but spending a lot of money. That said, you do need to pay attention and make certain that Facebook is spending your money wisely. You can see if you scroll down through this campaign, I have a bunch of ads that are turned off. So we started this off with quite a few ads and then split with MMA audience, New Jim Runner audience, MMA vice versa, just like we talked about. So if we look at like the lifetime of this campaign, you can see where ads have been turned off, maybe because they were not performing so well, or maybe some of them haven't performed very well in the last just the last 30 days. And so if you look at all of these ads and you change dynamically, you can cool down an ad for about 48 hours. So sometimes a little bit longer. So what I will do is click in my lifetime and look at, wow, wow, this one actually got a really awesome cost per result in my lifetime, 156, that's great. But I've turned it off. Why did I turn this ad off? So I'm going to hop and go to the last seven days and now let's see what this ad had spent. So this ad's had no spend in the last seven days. Let's see what it had in the last 14 days. In the last 14 days, it had 282 spend and no apps. So this is probably why this ad was turned off. That said, in the last seven days, it's really not spent anything. So it's probably safe to maybe give this ad another one if you know it's a good, if you know it typically got your results, which we saw that in the lifetime. It's had some great results. So let's turn this back on and let Facebook try to get us some more conversions with that ad because it did get good results. It got five results at 156. So it's a good ad for me. We've essentially given this ad some cool down time and now we're going to turn it back on and let Facebook hopefully route us some good traffic through it. So that is CBOs in a nutshell. I hope that you found this video useful. Definitely play around with them. Some of my experiences will tell you that taking a campaign that has already been running for a long time and converting them to CBO is an effective thing as well. So definitely look into doing that if you don't want to start a CBO campaign from scratch.
